subject: Cloud Computing
title: Chaos Engineering
description: A short lesson to help you apply Chaos Engineering.

hook.text: Netflix intentionally breaks its own production systems — during business hours. They call it Chaos Monkey.

buildup.text: Chaos engineering injects failures on purpose to test resilience. Kill a server. Slow the network. See what happens.

discovery.text: It reveals hidden dependencies and assumptions. Systems that seem robust often fail in unexpected ways.

twist.text: Most teams fear breaking things on purpose. But controlled failure tests beat discovering weaknesses during outages.

climax.text: AWS, Google, and Azure all recommend chaos engineering. If you haven't tested failure, you don't know if recovery works.

punchline.text: Break things on purpose. So they don't break by surprise.

quiz.question: What is the purpose of chaos engineering?
options: Deliberately injecting failures to discover weaknesses before real outages occur
Creating random bugs in source code to test developers
Shutting down all services to save cloud costs
